rationale: Author-of-record: the diff delivers plan Bead 1 Steps 1-5 and all four Acceptance Criteria exactly, nothing skipped. Step 1: Create(dir, CreateInput) (a plan-permitted signature) marshals a Panel via MarshalIndent in one write, plain values only, imports only stdlib (leaf check exits 0), bead_id json:null when nil, round+reviewed_head_sha in the same Panel by construction. Step 2: correct markers, in-memory validation before any write, legacy prepend, byte/CRLF-preserving splice on marker offsets, corrupt-marker rejection writing neither file, verdict files never touched, verbatim Your-job hard_block-as-top-level-sibling block. Step 4 doc names filename/pattern/consolidated/enum/hard_block with backtick tokens and ADR-0040 framing + a Maintenance Notes region (doc-sync). Steps 3+5 land all three name-exact tests (verified PASS) plus a doc-derived (not test-mirrored) schema pin. approve_threshold omitempty is the intended 109/ADR-0040 default-elision. Additions are benign and in-scope; no reinterpretation changes documented behavior.
findings: Step 1 faithful: CreateInput carries exactly BeadID *string/Spec/Target/Round/ExpectedReviewers/ApproveThresholdExpr/ReviewedHeadSHA; the plan explicitly permits the CreateInput variant while keeping the on-disk wire shape as Panel (create.go builds p := Panel{...} and MarshalIndents it). One os.WriteFile for panel.json; MkdirAll+both writes happen only after in-memory computation, satisfying the atomicity intent.

Step 2 faithful: spliceBriefHeader operates on strings.Index byte offsets (never line re-parse), so CRLF passes through; openCount==0&&closeCount==0 -> legacy prepend; openCount!=1||closeCount!=1 -> error before any write. Verified: marker_only_open and duplicated_markers subtests assert error + panel.json absent + BRIEF mtime/size/content unchanged.

Benign in-scope addition (not a defect): spliceBriefHeader adds a closeIdx<openIdx guard (closing marker precedes opening) beyond the plan's literal 'no-close / duplicated-pair' enumeration. It sits squarely inside the plan's 'ambiguous or corrupt' umbrella, create.go's doc comment enumerates it, and it changes no documented happy-path behavior.

Minor faithful+ reading: briefStubBody uses the four plan-named headings (Summary/Files in Scope/Prior-Round Asks/Lens) with <!-- TODO(skill) --> placeholder comments. Plan said headings 'left empty'; sections remain content-empty with a machine-readable marker for Bead 5 to fill. Not a scope deviation.

Immaterial reinterpretation: TestCreate_BRIEFMarkerEdgeCases uses t.Run subtests rather than a literal table slice ('a table' in the plan); functionally equivalent and all four documented cases (legacy/open-only/duplicated/CRLF) are covered and pass.

Steps 4+5 faithful: interfaces.md § Panel Artifact Schema backtick-quotes panel.json (== FileName), conforming R1-round-1.json/codex-correctness-round-2.json, the doc-labeled nonconforming R1-round-0.json, and consolidated-round-1.md (== ConsolidatedName(1)); TestPanelSchemaDoc_MatchesConstants re-derives its expectation from the doc's own tokens (not a test-held mirror) and adds the hard_block-not-adjacent-to-'finding' regression guard the plan asked for. Doc-sync also carries a Maintenance Notes region.

AC coverage confirmed live: go build ./internal/panel OK; the three name-exact tests PASS; deps=$(go list -deps ./internal/panel) && ! grep internal/config exits 0 (config-free leaf, AC10/R7b). git status clean after runs.
